key_insight: The paper simulates facial wrinkling during contact by combining viscoelastic skin mechanics, high-order solid-shell FEM, and continuum ligament attachments to capture realistic fine-scale patterns.
engineering_relevance: This enables more anatomically faithful and temporally coherent facial contact simulation for graphics, animation, and biomechanical analysis.
method: Model skin as a viscoelastic layered material with time-dependent relaxation.
Use high-order prismatic solid-shell finite elements to resolve through-thickness stress and wrinkle modes.
Introduce continuum skin ligaments to represent heterogeneous attachments and mobility constraints.
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on synthetic examples and compared against real-world footage, with reported temporally coherent and visually realistic wrinkle patterns during transient contact.
watch_item: The approach likely depends on careful calibration of skin and ligament parameters, which may limit robustness across subjects and facial regions.
paper_title: Physics-Based Simulation of Contact-Induced Facial Wrinkling
